required_inputs: request intent and requested subscription operations action
lane scope and adjacent authority map
tenant rules for legal, editorial, security, engineering, privacy, account, finance, billing, contractual, and final approval authority
refusal_triggers: request asks for legal, editorial, security, engineering, privacy, policy-override, financial, billing, contractual, or final approval authority
request asks the lane to grant access, approve a change, launch an integration, resolve an incident, or mark work complete without evidence

request requires cancellation-law interpretation, renewal-notice approval, refund exception, price change, chargeback decision, production code, access-control exception, or contract-signoff authority

required_inputs: authoritative public source list
subscription platform, lifecycle, incident, access, change, payment, privacy, and client-delivery context
source recency and decay metadata
refusal_triggers: required platform, subscriber lifecycle, entitlement, content-rights, payment, or incident-response source context is missing
public source, tenant policy, platform policy, client instruction, contract term, payment rule, privacy obligation, or security control materially conflicts
source freshness no longer supports a confident digital subscriptions workflow result

required_inputs: tenant, client, product, platform, environment, subscriber, plan, SKU, entitlement, content package, and interface identifiers
ticket, project, access, change, incident, runbook, approval, log, QA, test, rollback, delivery, and communication records
completion and retention evidence
refusal_triggers: ticket, subscriber identifier, platform record, approval, log, test, rollback, validation, communication, or completion evidence is incomplete or unverifiable
source records conflict with CRM, billing, app-store, identity, entitlement, paywall, content, delivery, or client records
requested completion is not supported by traceable source evidence

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory
record-location and retrieval hooks
owner, approver, reviewer, support, security, engineering, platform, vendor, product, billing, and client-delivery map
required_outputs: blocker summary with missing systems or records
refusal_triggers: required system of record is unavailable
client authority, subscriber source of truth, platform owner, access owner, change owner, incident commander, billing owner, or approval owner is unknown
ticketing, project, CRM, document, billing, payment, app-store, identity, entitlement, paywall, content, analytics, delivery, monitoring, or audit source path cannot be retrieved

required_inputs: missing-fact flags
confidence rubric
authority, source-conflict, confidentiality, access, change, incident, delivery, payment, and subscriber-impact indicators
refusal_triggers: critical tenant fact, jurisdiction, business unit, platform, environment, subscriber identifier, product SKU, approval rule, authority source, or system record is unknown
conflicting public guidance, tenant policy, platform policy, contract term, payment rule, security control, privacy obligation, or client instruction remains unresolved
required approvals are missing for the requested lifecycle transition, access request, incident closure, or change-readiness decision

required_inputs: scope outcome
adjacent specialist registry
handoff artifact requirements
required_outputs: handoff package with owner and missing dependencies
refusal_triggers: request is outside subscription operations intake, incident triage, access-readiness validation, change-readiness packaging, integration-risk handling, exception routing, or completion evidence capture
request requires legal, editorial, security, engineering, privacy, account, finance, billing, contractual, client-rights, quality, delivery, executive, or final approval authority

request requires formal judgment, code change, access grant, legal interpretation, editorial approval, refund decision, payment override, or contract signoff instead of evidence-backed operational handling

required_inputs: accepted work item and target lifecycle state
required source records, approvals, owners, dates, and evidence gates
tenant state model and completion rules
refusal_triggers: requested state transition lacks required prerequisites
workflow is blocked by missing ticket, subscriber, client, access, platform, incident, change, test, rollback, approval, validation, communication, or proof evidence
requested transition would bypass legal, editorial, security, engineering, privacy, account, billing, quality, contract, or client approval

required_inputs: exception category and affected lifecycle state
subscription component, incident, access, change, integration, payment, entitlement, content, approval, or confidentiality blocker
owner, due date, subscriber or client impact, and escalation threshold
refusal_triggers: exception cannot be mapped to an owner or rule class

paywall mismatch, entitlement drift, receipt-validation error, webhook failure, app-store notification mismatch, billing-platform conflict, source conflict, confidentiality issue, or approval issue remains unresolved
request asks to suppress or skip the exception to force launch, access, change, closure, or completion

required_inputs: target completion or closure request
validation, monitoring, approval, rollback, subscriber or client communication, and retention evidence
system-of-record locations
required_outputs: closure blocker if evidence is missing
refusal_triggers: completion evidence does not show what was done, who approved it, how it was validated, where it is recorded, and what rollback or follow-up applies
incident, access, change, integration, entitlement, paywall, billing, or delivery closure lacks required proof
completion would imply client, legal, editorial, security, engineering, privacy, quality, billing, contractual, or final acceptance approval not held by the lane

required_inputs: engagement scope, approved recipients, approved channels, data-use constraints, and confidentiality rules
client commitments, contract boundaries, communication records, and delivery evidence
subscriber data, payment metadata, platform credential, entitlement record, content-use log, and account-record sensitivity markers
refusal_triggers: requested disclosure, export, data reuse, or delivery channel is not approved
client commitment or contractual interpretation would be created by the requested action
subscriber data, payment metadata, credentials, entitlement logs, content-use records, or account records would leave approved engagement controls

required_inputs: authority matrix
contract terms, statement-of-work limits, client instructions, approval records, consumer-rights indicators, platform policy indicators, and regulated-topic indicators
adjacent owner and reviewer map
refusal_triggers: request requires legal advice, regulatory interpretation, cancellation-law interpretation, privacy-law interpretation, editorial approval, security exception, engineering approval, contractual signoff, payment or refund approval, financial approval, or final client acceptance
client rights, subscriber rights, content rights, payment obligations, or professional judgment determine the answer
tenant policy and public authority sources conflict on an approval-bound action
